# WHITE_PAPER_RIEMANN_Z5D_SYMMETRY_FALSIFICATION

## Experimental Data: Z5D Prime Predictions vs Ground Truth

This document contains experimental data comparing Z5D baseline predictions with ground truth prime values across different prime indices k.

### Data Format

Each row contains:
- `k`: prime index (1st prime, 2nd prime, etc.)
- `p_true`: ground truth prime number 
- `p_hat_z5d`: Z5D baseline prediction
- `err_abs`: absolute error (p_true - p_hat_z5d)
- `err_rel`: relative error ((p_true - p_hat_z5d)/p_true)

### Experimental Results

### Notes

- Data generated using Z5D baseline predictor with standard parameters
- Error patterns show systematic bias that may be correctable with O(1/log²k) terms
- Larger k values show decreasing absolute and relative errors as expected
- Sample size sufficient for statistical analysis with bootstrap validation
